title: RV8GR reset and clock bring-up
purpose: Package the first RV8GR bring-up proof: manual clock release, active-low reset, T0/T1/T2 sanity, and PC known-state policy.

chips.role: Ring feedback inverters for NOT(T0) and NOT(T1)

ports.name: CLK
ports.description: Manual or generated clock; Lab 01 creates the rising edge when the button is released.

ports.name: /RST
ports.description: Reset button net; LOW asynchronously clears U8 and U1-U4.

ports.name: PC_INC
ports.description: Integrated RV8GR bring-up count enable: T0 OR T1.

behavior.reset_release: Releasing /RST high does not advance state until the next clean rising CLK edge.
behavior.first_usable_phase: The first clean rising clock after reset enters T0 while PC remains 0x0000 because PC_INC was low before that edge.
behavior.phase_loop: Later clean rising edges repeat T0 -> T1 -> T2 with exactly one phase high.
behavior.pc_increment_rule: PC increments on edges that start while old T0 or old T1 is high; it holds on reset and old T2.
behavior.pc_unknown_policy: PC0..PC15 must never be X, Z, or unknown in reset idle, reset release, or post-clock bring-up observations.

lab01_clock.pressed: CLK low through normally-open push button
lab01_clock.debounce_note: 10k plus 100nF is a beginner RC debounce; tests require one clean logical edge per push.

timing.frequency_boundary: 5 MHz is functional simulation only; this package does not promote physical timing proof. Physical bring-up still needs R/C settling and deadband evidence on reset release, push-button clock edges, and PC counter carry paths.

guarantees: reset idle state clears ring and PC
reset release alone does not create a clock step
T0/T1/T2 are one-hot after clean pushes
PC has no X/Z/unknown values in bring-up observations
push switch, random 100 up to 500 ms, 50 kHz, 1 MHz, 2 MHz, and 5 MHz functional-only profiles are represented

audience: students ages 10-15, still useful for older learners
